name: FIFA World Cup 2026 one-sentence team summaries
attribution: Vlogbrothers (John Green): one sentence about each of the 48 teams. Auto-caption transcript, lightly edited for spelling of names.

summaries.CUW: The smallest nation ever to qualify for the World Cup — population about one-tenth the size of Indianapolis — Curaçao isn't going to win it, but they do have some of the best kits in the tournament.
summaries.HAI: Haiti had impressive qualification wins over Costa Rica and Nicaragua despite being unable to play any home games at home due to political instability, but unfortunately they got drawn into a group with Brazil and Morocco.
summaries.JOR: FIFA expanded to 48 nations hoping huge-population sides like China and India would qualify out of Asia, but instead Jordan reached their first ever World Cup — they won't win it, but with recent wins over Saudi Arabia and Egypt, don't overlook them.
summaries.IRQ: Iraq have made their first World Cup since 1986, thanks in part to former AFC Wimbledon star Ali Al-Hamadi, who scored a critical goal in qualifying.
summaries.NZL: New Zealand have Tim Payne, and with Tim Payne all things are possible.
summaries.UZB: Uzbekistan's men's team is called the White Wolves, but they have a tough group, so I don't expect them to get out of it.
summaries.QAT: Qatar have a relatively easy group, but they probably still won't get out of it.
summaries.CPV: World Cup debutants Cabo Verde have recent wins against Finland and Serbia, so they're at the tournament on merit and might even get out of their group.
summaries.KSA: Saudi Arabia will be looking to get out of their group for the first time since 1994 — when the tournament was also hosted in North America.
summaries.PAN: When Panama qualified in 2018 their president declared a national holiday, and it's impressive they qualified again in 2026, but I expect them to struggle, especially given their poor recent results.
summaries.RSA: South Africa, who famously hosted the tournament in 2010, are back for the first time since — they won't win it, but I think they'll surprise people by winning a game.
summaries.COD: The Democratic Republic of Congo won't win the tournament, but they have a single fan who stands at attention for their entire matches, and it's worth watching just for the TV cutaways to that guy.
summaries.IRN: Iran may well end up facing the US in the second round, and they'll believe they can upset the US as they did back in 1998.
summaries.AUS: Australia, the Socceroos — one of the best nicknames in the game — aren't expected to win any games, but I think they'll win one (possibly against the US) and make the knockout rounds.
summaries.BIH: Bosnia and Herzegovina beat Italy to qualify, and with 40-year-old Edin Džeko still banging in goals, I believe they'll make the knockout rounds.
summaries.KOR: South Korea have one of the great stars of global football in Son Heung-min, but on paper they're a little weaker than in past tournaments.
summaries.TUN: Tunisia don't have the star power of their North African neighbors Morocco, but they're known as the Eagles of Carthage, which is pretty badass.
summaries.ALG: Algeria just beat the Netherlands in a World Cup tune-up — a reminder that football isn't played by bookies or on spreadsheets, but by people on a pitch.
summaries.PAR: Paraguay are defensive-minded and tactically smart, but I'm unsure where their goals will come from — though that's also true of the others in their group: the US, Australia, and Turkey.
summaries.GHA: The Black Stars of Ghana have an aging golden generation, but they made the quarterfinals in 2010 and were one Luis Suárez handball away from the semis, so you never know.
summaries.EGY: Egypt have the legendary Mohamed Salah, through whom all things are possible.
summaries.CAN: Canada have an annoyingly good team at the moment — annoying because they already have universal healthcare, so being better than us at soccer feels a little punitive.
summaries.SCO: Scotland's Tartan Army will bring the vibes (no Scotland, no party), but with one of their star players injured I'm not convinced they'll bring the goals.
summaries.CZE: Nearly half the players selected for Czechia play for the same club, Slavia Prague, and that kind of cohesion can be really good for a World Cup team.
summaries.AUT: Austria have a good team with a stout defense, but they're extremely reliant on Marko Arnautović to score goals, so we'll see how they do.
summaries.MEX: Mexico are weaker than they've been in years, but it's hard to play them at home and they've got a weak group, so I expect them to make the round of 16.
summaries.CIV: The Ivory Coast just beat France in a World Cup tune-up, so who knows what's going to happen at this tournament.
summaries.USA: The US men's national team are only this high because I'm a USian and engage in the deranged belief they'll somehow make the quarterfinals on home soil, despite having a coach they clearly dislike and who seems to dislike them.
summaries.SEN: It would be a massive upset if Senegal won, but with stars like Sadio Mané they'll believe they can beat anyone — and they're not necessarily wrong, even though they just lost to the US in a tune-up.
summaries.SWE: Sweden have a fair amount of star power, but they got spanked by Norway recently and that worried me.
summaries.NOR: Norway have one of the world's best strikers in Erling Haaland, and I think they'll surprise some people — and not just because I'll be on vacation in Norway during part of the tournament.
summaries.URU: Uruguay have won the World Cup twice and are perpetual dark-horse candidates, but I just don't think this is going to be their year.
summaries.CRO: Croatia made the 2018 final and still have some of the same players, which is part of their problem, though they'll believe this aging golden generation can make one more run.
summaries.TUR: I'm convinced Turkey, having barely qualified, will beat the United States and win their group, but then they'll run into the likes of Brazil or France.
summaries.SUI: Switzerland tend to play somewhat uninspired but effective football that neutralizes their opponents — because, you know, they're neutral.
summaries.BEL: Belgium will be wondering whether they wasted their golden generation of footballers, and I think this tournament will prove that yes, they have.
summaries.GER: Germany are pre-tournament favorites to at least make the quarterfinals, but I'm not even convinced they're going to win their group.
summaries.ECU: I think Germany's group will actually be won by Ecuador, who with their stout defense are one of my sleeper picks to go very far — until they run into an unstoppable force called France.
summaries.JPN: A shout-out to Japan, the Samurai Blue, who should be very good this year, as evidenced by the fact they recently beat England.
summaries.NED: The Netherlands are probably the best footballing nation never to win the World Cup, and they'll believe they can go far with stars like Cody Gakpo and Virgil van Dijk.
summaries.POR: In my opinion Portugal are actually better when they don't play their aging superstar Cristiano Ronaldo, but I suspect they're going to play him anyway.
summaries.MAR: In 2022 Morocco became the first African team ever to reach the semi-finals, and they could repeat that trick again.
summaries.COL: Colombia are my dark-horse pick to win the World Cup this year, with electrifying players like Luis Díaz and a wonderfully creative midfield.
summaries.BRA: Five-time winner Brazil has been very supportive of my books and is therefore one of my favorite national teams, and could go really far partly because expectations are, for once, a little lower.
summaries.ENG: England won the World Cup in 1966 and haven't since, and they have the players to do it this year — but will they overcome the weight of expectation?
summaries.ARG: Lionel Messi's Argentina won the World Cup last time out and will believe they can again, not least because of their stalwart defense and their extraordinary goalkeeper.
summaries.FRA: Kylian Mbappé will get all the headlines, but France are stacked in every position, and if they don't at least make the final this tournament will feel like a disappointment to them.
summaries.ESP: And finally there's Spain, with their rhythmic, metronomic football and ridiculously talented player pool — including 18-year-old Lamine Yamal, poised to be the breakout star of this World Cup if he's healthy.
